justification: Census tract encodes residential segregation patterns. Highly correlated with race/ethnicity due to historical redlining. Treat as High proxy risk; monitor SHAP rank and correlation.
Direct demographic encoding of tract-level minority share. Near-perfect proxy for race if used as a continuous feature.
MSA/MD encodes regional income and demographic composition. Correlated with race/ethnicity due to geographic sorting.
Tract-level income reflects structural income inequality. Correlated with race; use with caution.
Relative income of tract vs. MSA; encodes neighborhood affluence which correlates with racial composition.
Applicant income is a legitimate creditworthiness indicator but correlates with race due to structural inequity. Retained; monitored.
Age is a protected attribute under ECOA. Monitor model reliance.
State encodes regional demographic composition. May proxy for race in some contexts.
County-level demographic encoding similar to MSA.
